reasoning: The UK Supreme Court explicitly ruled in November 2022 that the Scottish Parliament does not have the legal competence to hold an independence referendum without a Section 30 order from the UK Government. The UK Government, now led by a Labour majority firmly opposed to independence, has completely ruled out granting such an order. Moreover, the SNP suffered severe electoral losses in the July 2024 UK General Election, which largely dismantled the prospect of utilizing any near-term election as a 'de facto' referendum. Although the background text mentions a single poll showing an 11-point lead for independence, the Scottish Government respects the rule of law and lacks any viable legal route or immediate political capital to formally schedule a referendum date. Furthermore, given the forecast date is merely two months prior to the July 2025 end date, there is scarcely enough time or political momentum for such an unprecedented, legally-doomed announcement. There is essentially no chance of an official independence referendum date being scheduled unilaterally before July 2025.
